priceSnapshot.note: BTC lost the $65,500–$65,380 shelf that held overnight and printed ~$65,050 after opening $66,081. Driver is an oil shock: Brent broke above $100 (+~6.6%) on attacks against Saudi tankers / US-Iran tensions, reviving inflation fears and pressuring risk. Still inside the broader $64,000–$66,800 range but the tactical bull shelf is gone.

reduceIf.confirmation: 1H close below $64,062 — loses the range/invalidation floor; scale the reduce toward EXIT

addIf.confirmation: 4H close back above $65,500 reclaims the lost shelf — re-add the trimmed tranche

decisionBox.changed: The morning's $65,380 REDUCE trigger FIRED: BTC closed the hour below the shelf on the Brent>$100 oil shock and traded to ~$65,050. The tactical trim is now live; the long-term spot core still holds above the $64,062 invalidation.

atr.source: Not verified this run (exchange APIs blocked from sandbox). Prior verified daily ATR(14) ~$1,553 (~2.36%) on Jul 22 — deliberately NOT carried into any calculation here.

timeframes.structure: Impulsive break below the $65,500/$65,380 shelf on the oil headline; lower-highs, sellers in control intraday.
timeframes.source: Price action per CoinDesk / crypto.news (Jul 23); intraday indicator table not verified (exchange APIs blocked)

timeframes.structure: Lost the $65,380 shelf on a 1H close — that level now caps as resistance; next demand $64,800–$64,500.
Broke the lower edge of the three-session compression; still above the $64,062 range floor, so this reads as a range-low test rather than a confirmed trend break.
Constructive-but-capped July repair now pulling back from the $66,000 cap; daily EMA200 still sits far above price (higher-TF downtrend intact). RSI not re-verified this run.
timeframes.source: Daily RSI not re-verified this run (morning Investing.com read was ~61.4; not carried forward)

scalpContext.note: Scalp only the edges — live signals come from the chart, not this report.

etfFlows.note: Last confirmed streak = 6 sessions / ~$930M net through Jul 22 (SoSoValue, $203.1M on Jul 21). Jul 23's flow prints after the US close and is not yet verifiable; the intraday risk-off pressures a 7th-day continuation but the structural bid remains the bulls' best card.

liquidations.note: Sub-$65,500 break flushed leveraged longs, but a clean, current long/short split was not verifiable this run (CoinGlass not re-scraped). Not fabricating a number.

indices.note: Down -1.76% intraday Jul 23 (last $692.95, prev close $705.35) as oil's climb rekindles inflation fears; day's range $687.79–$698.66.

dcaSignal.note: Computed from trailing daily closes through the last completed session (Jul 22); today's ~1.8% intraday drop would push the read cheaper at Jul 23's close. Relative-cheapness for monthly DCA, not a bottom call.

indices.note: Down -1.17% intraday Jul 23 (last $678.96, prev close $687.03); day's range $675.76–$682.55. Broad market pulling back off the top of its monthly range on the oil/yields risk-off.

dcaSignal.note: Computed from trailing daily closes through the last completed session (Jul 22); today's ~1.2% intraday drop would push the read cheaper at Jul 23's close. Relative-cheapness for monthly DCA, not a bottom call.

indices.note: StockAnalysis feed is STALE again — last print is dated Jun 18, 2026 ($101.73, prev close $100.24), not a current Jul 23 session. Per the data-integrity rule, price/changePct are null and dcaSignal is omitted.

calibration.rollingRecord: Range-with-bearish-tilt call validated intraday; the morning's $65,380 reduce level triggered as flagged.
calibration.note: The morning report set position HOLD with reduceIf a 1H close below $65,380. BTC lost that shelf on the Brent>$100 oil shock and traded to ~$65,050, so the reduce trigger FIRED. Acting on it (trimming the tactical/position sleeve at ~$65,380) would have helped versus riding the drop toward $65,000, so actingHelped = true. The long-term spot core still holds above the $64,062 invalidation.
